example_name: DeepSeek AI Model Content Moderation Adjustments
example_background: DeepSeek, a Chinese AI company, released a large language model that generated content on sensitive political topics, including discussions around historical events and government policies. example_name: Alibaba Group Holding Limited Data Security Case
example_background: Alibaba Group Holding Limited, a major e-commerce platform operator in China, collected and utilized user personal information extensively through its online services. example_name: Jack Ma Ant Group IPO Suspension
example_background: In October 2020, Ant Group, the financial technology arm of Alibaba founded by Jack Ma, prepared for what was set to be the world's largest initial public offering on the Shanghai and Hong Kong stock exchanges, valued at over $34 billion. Jack Ma delivered a speech criticizing China's financial regulators for their outdated approach to risk management and supervision, stating that regulators were stifling innovation by focusing on safety over growth. Days before the planned IPO, Chinese authorities halted the listing, citing concerns over financial stability, consumer protection, data security, and potential systemic risks posed by Ant Group's rapid expansion into consumer lending, wealth management, and payment services without adequate capital buffers or risk controls. This event occurred amid a broader regulatory crackdown on big tech firms in China, highlighting tensions between private sector innovation and state oversight of public financial security.
process_and_outcome: The China Banking and Insurance Regulatory Commission (CBIRC), People's Bank of China (PBOC), and China Securities Regulatory Commission (CSRC) conducted reviews and suspended the IPO indefinitely. Ant Group was ordered to restructure its business model, including retreating from certain credit services and increasing capital reserves. Jack Ma retreated from public view temporarily, and Ant's valuation dropped significantly from $315 billion to around $70 billion. In April 2021, regulators imposed a record $2.8 billion fine on Ant Group for regulatory violations, with ongoing supervision to ensure compliance.
involved_parties: China Banking and Insurance Regulatory Commission (CBIRC)

example_background: Youku, operated by Alibaba Group, hosted online A/V programming generated by deep synthesis services that failed to comply with labeling requirements. Technical supporters provided tools for creating synthetic news broadcasts and cultural event recaps, which were published without watermarks or disclosures. This content, part of online publication services, garnered significant engagement before regulatory alerts. The platform's oversight mechanisms did not enforce concurrent compliance with news publication and cultural departments, allowing the material to proliferate.
process_and_outcome: NRTA and CAC imposed a 10 million RMB penalty on Youku and ordered immediate content removal. Alibaba committed to a 6-month remediation plan, including third-party audits and enhanced provider contracts.

example_background: Kuaishou, a short-video platform, established a multi-departmental compliance unit to oversee deep synthesis services in online cultural and A/V activities. Providers and technical supporters were onboarded through a certification process requiring proof of labeling capabilities. All synthetic content for publication underwent dual review by platform moderators and external cultural experts, ensuring alignment with radio, television, news, and tourism provisions.
process_and_outcome: Ministry of Culture and Tourism audit validated the framework, resulting in zero violations recorded. Kuaishou was highlighted in national reports as a best practice example.

example_background: Xiaohongshu, a lifestyle and e-commerce platform with video features, dealt with deep synthesis tools used for creating virtual influencer content in online publication services. Technical supporters enabled generation of A/V clips simulating cultural events and product endorsements without labels, integrating into user feeds as authentic cultural activities. The platform's growth in video content amplified the reach of this unlabeled material.
process_and_outcome: Joint enforcement by publication and tourism authorities led to content takedowns and a 3 million RMB fine. Xiaohongshu upgraded its detection AI and restricted uncertified providers.

example_name: Mango TV Deep Synthesis Best Practices
example_background: Mango TV, a Hunan Broadcasting System platform, integrated deep synthesis compliance into its production pipeline for online A/V programming. Technical supporters for virtual hosts and effects were contractually bound to department-specific standards for news, culture, and broadcasting. All content received pre-publication certification, with transparent labeling displayed to viewers.
process_and_outcome: NRTA compliance certification granted in 2024, with the platform serving as a training case for other broadcasters. No issues identified in routine inspections.

example_background: Weibo, a microblogging platform with video capabilities, permitted deep synthesis services for user-posted cultural clips and A/V content without enforcing concurrent departmental rules. Technical tools allowed synthesis of celebrity endorsements and event footage, published as online cultural activities reaching hundreds of millions of users.
process_and_outcome: CAC ordered removal of 20,000+ posts and fined Weibo 8 million RMB. The platform introduced mandatory provider registration and labeling enforcement.

example_background: In early 2023, Chinese authorities identified an online platform operating on WeChat that enabled users to upload photographs of women and generate highly realistic nude videos using deep synthesis technology. The service processed over 5,700 orders, producing immersive virtual scenes depicting explicit content that users could view and interact with. The platform collected fees ranging from 30 to 80 yuan per video, targeting primarily young women whose images were sourced from social media. This operation involved automated tools to swap faces onto adult video bodies, creating content that appeared indistinguishable from real footage. The platform's activities gained attention after public complaints from affected individuals whose likenesses were used without consent, highlighting the rapid proliferation of such services on mainstream apps before detection.
process_and_outcome: Police in Beijing investigated the case, leading to the criminal detention of three individuals operating the platform. The case was classified under relevant internet content regulations, resulting in the platform's immediate shutdown, removal of related content, and public warnings issued by authorities to deter similar operations.

The company failed to disclose the synthetic origin of the content, leading to public confusion about authenticity.
process_and_outcome: Cyberspace Administration of China (CAC) conducted an inspection, issued administrative penalties including fines and ordered content removal. The company was required to implement disclosure mechanisms for all future synthetic content.
